title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
During a gathering, thirteen-year-old Joyce Reynolds claims she witnessed a murder when she was very young. Adults including Miss Whitaker, Royana Drecker, Anne, and Cassie dismiss her story as nonsense. Joyce explains she did not contact the police because she only realized the event was a murder months ago after hearing someone else mention it. When Beatrice asks for details regarding the victim and the killer, Joyce refuses to answer because she finds Beatrice annoying.

As the group prepares for an apple-biting game, a debate ensues regarding whether a galvanized pail or a plastic bucket is better; they conclude the metal pail is superior despite the plastic bucket being more attractive. Seeking privacy after being teased about her love for apples, Ariadne Oliver heads toward the "Apple Orchard" house. On her way, she encounters a fifteen-year-old boy and a twelve-year-old girl embracing against a door. When Oliver asks to pass, the lovers reluctantly separate and call her selfish. Oliver enters the house and locks the door behind her.

Later, Oliver discusses the complexities of parties with Judith Butler, who suggests that Royana Drecker is an excellent organizer. At 7:30 PM, the party begins, featuring decorations of red, blue, and yellow pumpkins. Drecker announces a program including a broomstick competition, cutting cornbread in a greenhouse, an apple-biting contest, dancing, a mirror ritual in the small study, dinner, and a dragon hunt. During the broomstick competition, Drecker explains she is using small brooms to ensure prizes are distributed evenly among the children. The festivities continue with the apple-biting game and a performance by Mrs. Goodbody, a local cleaning woman playing a witch. The witch performs a ritual for Beatrice, instructing her to hold a mirror and wait for the lights to go out to see the face of her future husband.
